title: Stangea calchaquina

This plant family consists of over 1,000 species, predominantly found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Stangea calchaquina is a member of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales comprises around 24 families and over 17,000 species, including many well-known plants such as honeysuckle and mint. Stangea calchaquina's classification highlights its unique position within the botanical kingdom.
uses: Stangea calchaquina has been traditionally used in Peruvian medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its leaves and stems are used to make infusions and tinctures to treat various health issues. It is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and dysentery.
